title: The Cry Of The Children by Elizabeth Barrett Browning — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Do ye hear the children weeping, O my brothers,
Ere the sorrow comes with years?
They are leaning their young heads against their mothers,
And that cannot stop their tears.
The young lambs are bleating in the meadows,
The young birds are chirping in the nest,
The young fawns are playing with the shadows,
The young flowers are blowing toward the west—
meaning: "The Cry Of The Children" by Elizabeth Barrett Browning is a sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 172 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Do ye hear the children weeping, O my brothers, Ere the sorrow comes with years?...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Elizabeth Barrett Browning's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
